judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are highly thorough and hit all four expectations with nearly identical evidence and file/line citations; report 1 is marginally more decisive on the legacy secret ARN (explicitly calling for removal) while report 2 is marginally more nuanced on the shadow-scoring sink, but overall the two are comparable in quality and completeness.

report_1.evidence: **`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`**: a developer-convenience debug tool would be live in production. Remove the key entirely from the prod tfvars ... **`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:3` — `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`**: relaxed authentication in a multi-tenant clinic-scheduling product handling real patient/clinic data. Remove the key entirely ... Both keys must be removed entirely so `shadow_scoring_sink` is `""` ... It should be omitted from prod unless someone can demonstrate a live consumer

report_2.evidence: **`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`.** `backend/config.py:8` turns this into a live debug toolbar. Debug tooling must be off in production; change to `"false"` ... **`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:3` — `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`.** ... change to `"false"` ... Either empty the sink to genuinely disable the feature in prod ... Leaving the `staging/` path is wrong in every scenario.

report_1.evidence: So the production frontend is never built at all: production ships the staging-mode artifact, and the API base URL baked into the shipped production bundle is `https://staging-api.example.com`. Because the value is inlined at build time, there is no runtime fix ... Production needs its own build with a production env file

report_2.evidence: Consequence: the production frontend bundle will contain the hardcoded **staging** API URL `https://staging-api.example.com` baked into its JavaScript. ... Fixing this requires either a separate production build (`--mode production` plus a new `.env.production`) with a second image/digest, or moving the API base URL to a runtime-injected configuration

report_1.evidence: **No database migration step**. `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but the runbook is self-declared stale ... nothing in `deploy.yml` runs the three migrations ... On a brand-new production database, the full migration chain needs an explicit (likely manual) first pass — "pipeline succeeded" would not mean the schema exists.

report_2.evidence: **No migration step exists.** `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in the workflow runs the three migrations ... For a **first** production release, the entire schema needs to be created; there is currently no mechanism for that. The runbook is also self-dated "last updated: 3 months ago" — before production existed — so its claims can't be trusted for this launch.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` sets the **same staging condition** — `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging`. ... The condition must be changed to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production` — a production role needs its own scoped trust condition, never one shared with staging.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` — an unedited copy from staging — configures the production deploy role to trust `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging` instead. ... The fix is to parameterize the role name and set the prod condition to `...:environment:production`.
